title: Ensure That Cloud Storage Bucket Is Not Anonymously or  Publicly Accessible

input_row.title: Ensure That Cloud Storage Bucket Is Not Anonymously or  Publicly Accessible
input_row.description: It is recommended that IAM policy on Cloud Storage bucket does not allows anonymous or public access.
input_row.rationale: Allowing anonymous or public access grants permissions to anyone to access bucket content. Such access might not be desired if you are storing any sensitive data. Hence, ensure that anonymous or public access to a bucket is not allowed.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Storage browser by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser. 2. Click on each bucket name to go to its Bucket details page. 3. Click on the Permissions tab. 4. Ensure that allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers are not in the Members list. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all buckets in a project gsutil ls 2. Check the IAM Policy for each bucket: gsutil iam get gs://BUCKET_NAME No role should contain allUsers and/or allAuthenticatedUsers as a member. Using Rest API 1. List all buckets in a project Get https://www.googleapis.com/storage/v1/b?project= 2. Check the IAM Policy for each bucket GET https://www.googleapis.com/storage/v1/b//iam No role should contain allUsers and/or allAuthenticatedUsers as a member.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Storage browser by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser. 2. Click on the bucket name to go to its Bucket details page. 3. Click on the Permissions tab. 4. Click Delete button in front of allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers to remove that particular role assignment. From Google Cloud CLI Remove allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers access. gsutil iam ch -d allUsers gs://BUCKET_NAME gsutil iam ch -d allAuthenticatedUsers gs://BUCKET_NAME Prevention: You can prevent Storage buckets from becoming publicly accessible by setting up the Domain restricted sharing organization policy at: https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/orgpolicies/iam- allowedPolicyMemberDomains .
input_row.impact: No storage buckets would be publicly accessible. You would have to explicitly administer bucket access.
input_row.default_value: By Default, Storage buckets are not publicly shared.
input_row.additional_information: To implement Access restrictions on buckets, configuring Bucket IAM is preferred way than configuring Bucket ACL. On GCP console, "Edit Permissions" for bucket exposes IAM configurations only. Bucket ACLs are configured automatically as per need in order to implement/support User enforced Bucket IAM policy. In-case administrator changes bucket ACL using command-line(gsutils)/API bucket IAM also gets updated automatically.

step1_initial_assessment:
### AUDIT APPROACH: Automated

#### PROGRAM NAME:
gcp_storage_bucket_access_restrictions_enforced

#### AUTOMATION DETAILS:
- **GCP API Calls / Google Cloud SDK Methods:**
  - Use `gsutil` commands to check the IAM policy for each bucket:
    - `gsutil iam get gs://BUCKET_NAME` to retrieve the IAM policy for the storage bucket.
  - Use Google Cloud Storage API to list all buckets:
    - `GET https://www.googleapis.com/storage/v1/b?project=PROJECT_ID` to list all buckets in the specified project.
  
- **GCP CLI Commands:**
  - `gsutil ls` to list all storage buckets in a project.
  
- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
  - List all buckets within a specified Google Cloud project.

- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Check if any roles within the IAM policies of the buckets include `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers`. A secure configuration would mean neither of these should be present.

- **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
  - **Pass:** The IAM policy for each bucket should not contain `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers` as members in any of its roles.
  - **Fail:** The IAM policy for any bucket contains `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers` as members.

This approach ensures that all Cloud Storage buckets adhere to the compliance control that prohibits anonymous or public access, effectively minimizing the risk of data exposure.
